sentences.base_language: Sami had been planning the move for months and the day had finally arrived.
sentences.target_language: كان سامي يخطِّط للانتقال منذ أشهرٍ، وقد حلَّ اليوم المنتظَر أخيراً.
sentences.romanized: Kāna Sāmī yukhaṭṭiṭu lil-intiqāli mundhu ashhur, wa-qad ḥalla l-yawmu l-muntaẓaru akhīran.
sentences.grammarNote:
**كان يخطِّط** is كان + مضارع expressing past progressive/habitual — he had been planning (over an extended period); this construction describes an action that was ongoing or repeated in the past before another event.
**المنتظَر** is a passive participle (Form VIII مفتعَل pattern of نظر: انتظر) used as an attributive adjective — the awaited day; Form VIII passive participles are extremely productive in MSA as nominal modifiers.

sentences.base_language: His friends arrived early in the morning to help carry boxes and furniture down to the moving truck.
sentences.target_language: وصل أصدقاؤه مبكِّرين في الصباح ليساعدوا في حمل الصناديق والأثاث إلى سيارة النقل.
sentences.romanized: Waṣala aṣdiqāʾuhu mubakkirīna fī ṣ-ṣabāḥi li-yusāʿidū fī ḥamli ṣ-ṣanādīqi wa-l-athāthi ilā sayyārati n-naql.
sentences.grammarNote:
**ليساعدوا** is لام التعليل + مضارع منصوب (subjunctive) expressing purpose — in order to help; the subjunctive marker drops the final نون of the plural verb يساعدون, giving يساعدوا.
**الصناديق** is the broken plural of صندوق (box/crate) following the pattern فعاليل, one of the quadrilateral broken plural patterns used for four-consonant roots.

sentences.base_language: After several trips up and down the stairs, everyone's arms and legs were aching.
sentences.target_language: بعد جولاتٍ عديدةٍ صعوداً وهبوطاً على الدرج، كانت أذرع الجميع وأرجلهم تؤلمهم.
sentences.romanized: Baʿda jawlātin ʿadīdatin ṣuʿūdan wa-hubūṭan ʿalā d-daraji, kānat adhruʿu l-jamīʿi wa-arjuluhum tuʾlimuhum.
sentences.grammarNote:
**صعوداً وهبوطاً** are masdars (verbal nouns) used as adverbial ḥāl — going up and going down; this use of the masdar in the accusative without a verb is a hallmark of MSA formal style.
**أذرع** is the broken plural of ذِراع (arm) following the pattern أفعُل, while **أرجل** is the broken plural of رِجْل (leg/foot) on the pattern أفعُل — both are among the common broken plurals for body-part nouns.

sentences.base_language: The old apartment looked strangely empty once everything had been removed.
sentences.target_language: بدت الشقة القديمة فارغةً بشكلٍ غريبٍ بعد أن أُزيل كلُّ شيءٍ منها.
sentences.romanized: Badat ash-shaqqqatu l-qadīmatu fārighatan bi-shaklin gharībin baʿda an uzīla kullu shayʾin minhā.
sentences.grammarNote:
**أُزيل** is Form IV passive (أُفعِل pattern of زال: أزال) meaning was removed; the passive is formed by changing the vowel pattern of the active (أَزَالَ becomes أُزِيلَ), and no agent is expressed — a common narrative device to keep focus on the action.
**بدت فارغةً** uses بدا as a sister verb of كان, taking its subject in the nominative and its predicate خبر in the accusative — فارغةً carries the accusative tanwīn as the predicate.

sentences.base_language: Sami took one last look around and felt a mix of nostalgia and excitement about the future.
sentences.target_language: ألقى سامي نظرةً أخيرةً على المكان وأحسَّ بمزيجٍ من الحنين والإثارة تجاه المستقبل.
sentences.romanized: Alqā Sāmī naẓratan akhīratan ʿalā l-makāni wa-aḥassa bi-mazījin min al-ḥanīni wa-l-ithārati tijāha l-mustaqbal.
sentences.grammarNote:
**ألقى نظرةً** is the fixed MSA idiom to cast a glance / take a look; ألقى is Form IV of لقي (أفعل causative), and نظرةً is the masdar of نظر used as a direct object — casting a single act of looking.
**أحسَّ بـ** (Form IV of حسَّ) requires the preposition بـ to introduce what is felt; this verb-preposition collocation is obligatory in MSA and cannot be omitted.

sentences.base_language: The new apartment was on the third floor of a quiet building in a lively neighborhood.
sentences.target_language: كانت الشقة الجديدة في الطابق الثالث من بناءٍ هادئٍ في حيٍّ صاخبٍ.
sentences.romanized: Kānat ash-shaqqatu l-jadīdatu fī ṭ-ṭābiqi th-thālithi min binā'in hādi'in fī ḥayyin ṣākhib.
sentences.grammarNote:
**الطابق الثالث** uses the ordinal adjective ثالث (third), derived from the cardinal ثلاثة on the pattern فاعل; ordinal numbers above ten use the cardinal form in MSA, but those from two to ten follow this فاعل pattern.
**حيٍّ صاخبٍ** are both in the genitive with double tanwīn (tanwīn al-jarr), showing full case marking; the indefinite genitive is required after the preposition في, and the adjective صاخب agrees in case and indefiniteness.

sentences.base_language: When they arrived, a neighbor from the adjacent apartment came out to introduce herself and welcome Sami.
sentences.target_language: حين وصلوا، خرجت جارةٌ من الشقة المجاورة لتعرِّف بنفسها وترحِّب بسامي.
sentences.romanized: Ḥīna waṣalū, kharajat jāratun min ash-shaqqati l-mujāwirati li-tuʿarrifa bi-nafsihā wa-turaḥḥiba bi-Sāmī.
sentences.grammarNote:
**لتعرِّف بنفسها** uses لام التعليل + subjunctive of Form II verb عرَّف (to introduce); Form II (فعَّل) is the causative/intensive of عرف — to make known — and بنفسها (reflexive by herself) signals self-introduction.
**المجاورة** is a Form III active participle (مفاعِلة of جاور) meaning adjacent/neighboring; Form III (فاعَل) expresses reciprocal proximity — sharing a border — and the participle functions as an attributive adjective here.

sentences.base_language: She told him there was a good bakery and a small vegetable market just around the corner.
sentences.target_language: أخبرته أنَّ ثمةَ مخبزاً جيِّداً وسوقاً صغيرةً للخضروات على بُعد خطواتٍ.
sentences.romanized: Akhbarathu anna thammata makhbazan jayyidan wa-sūqan ṣaghīratan lil-khuḍrūwāti ʿalā buʿdi khuṭuwāt.
sentences.grammarNote:
**أنَّ ثمةَ** uses أنَّ (the strong complementizer) followed by the existential particle ثمةَ (there is/are); ثمةَ is the MSA formal equivalent of هناك in existential sentences, and what follows it is the subject in the nominative — مخبزٌ/سوقٌ.
**مخبزاً** is the masdar/nomen loci pattern مَفعَل of خبز (to bake), meaning a place of baking — bakery; nouns of place on the pattern مَفعَل/مَفعِل are a productive derivational category in Arabic.

sentences.base_language: Unloading the truck and carrying everything up the stairs took the rest of the afternoon.
sentences.target_language: استغرق تفريغُ الشاحنة وحمل كلِّ شيءٍ إلى الأعلى بقيةَ فترة الظهيرة.
sentences.romanized: Istaghraqa tafrīghu sh-shāḥinati wa-ḥamlu kulli shayʾin ilā l-aʿlā baqiyyata fatrati ẓ-ẓuhīra.
sentences.grammarNote:
**تفريغُ الشاحنة** is an إضافة where the masdar تفريغ (Form II masdar of فرَّغ, to unload/empty) governs its object الشاحنة in the genitive; masdars in إضافة retain their verbal valency — the truck is the logical object of the unloading.
**استغرق** is Form X of غَرِق (to drown/sink), meaning to consume/take up (time); Form X (استفعل) here expresses the sense of drawing something into itself — the task consumed all the afternoon hours.

sentences.base_language: By the time the heavy furniture was in place, everyone was exhausted and drenched in sweat.
sentences.target_language: حين استقرَّ الأثاث الثقيل في مكانه، كان الجميع منهَكين ومبللين بالعرق.
sentences.romanized: Ḥīna staqarra l-athāthu th-thaqīlu fī makānihi, kāna l-jamīʿu munhakīna wa-muballalīna bil-ʿaraq.
sentences.grammarNote:
**استقرَّ** is Form X of قَرَّ (to be settled/cool), meaning to become settled/established in a place; Form X here expresses the attainment of a stable state — the furniture finally came to rest.
**منهَكين** is the plural accusative of the passive participle مُنهَك (Form IV of هكَّ: أنهك, to exhaust); **مُبلَّلين** is the plural accusative passive participle of Form II بلَّل (to soak/drench); both carry accusative ـين as predicates of كان.

sentences.base_language: Sami ordered several large pizzas to thank his friends for all their help.
sentences.target_language: طلب سامي عدة بيتزات كبيرةٍ شكراً لأصدقائه على كلِّ مساعدتهم.
sentences.romanized: Ṭalaba Sāmī ʿiddata bītzātin kabīratin shukran li-aṣdiqāʾihi ʿalā kulli musāʿadatihim.
sentences.grammarNote:
**شكراً لـ** is the masdar شكر used in the accusative as an adverbial of purpose/reason — in thanks to; this masdar construction (masdar منصوب as مفعول لأجله) expresses the motive for the action without a full verb.
**عدة** (several/a number of) is a noun in إضافة with its مضاف إليه in the genitive; unlike كم, عدة always takes a plural genitive مضاف إليه and itself carries the case required by its position in the sentence.

sentences.base_language: They sat on the floor surrounded by boxes and ate hungrily, too tired to unpack the dishes.
sentences.target_language: جلسوا على الأرض محاطين بالصناديق وأكلوا بنَهَمٍ، إذ كانوا أشدَّ تعباً من أن يفكِّروا في إخراج الصحون.
sentences.romanized: Jalasū ʿalā l-arḍi muḥāṭīna biṣ-ṣanādīqi wa-akalū bi-nahamin, idh kānū ashadda taʿaban min an yufakkirū fī ikhrāji ṣ-ṣuḥūn.
sentences.grammarNote:
**محاطين** is the plural accusative passive participle of Form IV أحاط (to surround), functioning as a ḥāl — describing the state of the subject while sitting; ḥāl participles agree with their referent in gender and number.
**أشدَّ تعباً من أن** uses the elative أشدّ (more intense) in the pattern أشدَّ + masdar/نكرة + من أن + subjunctive — too exhausted to; this is the standard MSA construction for expressing excess that prevents an action.

sentences.base_language: After his friends left, Sami stood alone in the middle of the new space for the first time.
sentences.target_language: بعد أن ودَّع سامي أصدقاءه، وقف وحده في وسط الشقة الجديدة للمرة الأولى.
sentences.romanized: Baʿda an waddaʿa Sāmī aṣdiqāʾahu, waqafa waḥdahu fī wasaṭi sh-shaqqati l-jadīdati lil-marrati l-ūlā.
sentences.grammarNote:
**ودَّع** is Form II of وَدَع (to bid farewell); Form II (فعَّل) here is intensive/transitive — to formally say goodbye to someone — distinguished from Form I which is rarely used in this sense in MSA.
**للمرة الأولى** uses the feminine ordinal الأولى (first, feminine) agreeing with المرة (time/instance, feminine); الأولى is the irregular feminine elative of أوَّل, derived from the root وأل.

sentences.base_language: The rooms felt unfamiliar and full of echoes but somehow also full of possibility.
sentences.target_language: بدت الغرف غريبةً ومليئةً بالأصداء، غير أنها كانت في الوقت ذاته مليئةً بإمكاناتٍ لا حصر لها.
sentences.romanized: Badat al-ghurufu gharībatan wa-malīʾatan bil-aṣdāʾi, ghayra annaha kānat fī l-waqti dhātihi malīʾatan bi-imkānātin lā ḥaṣra lahā.
sentences.grammarNote:
**غير أنَّ** is a concessive connector meaning however/nevertheless; غير functions as a noun meaning other than, and the following أنَّ clause is its مضاف إليه — a formal MSA alternative to لكنَّ.
**لا حصر لها** uses لا النافية للجنس (the lā of categorical negation) with a masdar حصر (counting/enumeration); this formula — لا + masdar + لـ + pronoun — is an MSA idiom meaning innumerable / beyond counting.

sentences.base_language: He decided to start by unpacking the kitchen — that always made a new place feel more like home.
sentences.target_language: قرَّر أن يبدأ بترتيب المطبخ — فذلك دائماً ما يجعل المكان الجديد يبدو أشبهَ بالبيت.
sentences.romanized: Qarrara an yabdaʾa bi-tartībi l-maṭbakhi — fa-dhālika dāʾiman mā yajʿalu l-makāna l-jadīda yabdū ashjaha bil-bayt.
sentences.grammarNote:
**قرَّر أن يبدأ** uses قرَّر (Form II of قرَّ: to decide) followed by أن + مضارع منصوب (subjunctive), the standard MSA pattern for volitional verbs governing a following infinitival complement.
**أشبه بـ** is the elative of شبيه/مشابه (similar/like), meaning more resembling / more like; the elative أفعل pattern of ش-ب-ه, followed by the obligatory preposition بـ, is a fixed MSA comparative construction.

sentences.base_language: Finding the box labeled 'bedding' took longer than expected and nearly caused a moment of panic.
sentences.target_language: استلزم العثور على الصندوق المكتوب عليه 'مفروشات' وقتاً أطول مما كان متوقَّعاً وكاد يُسبِّب لحظةً من الذعر.
sentences.romanized: Istalzama l-ʿuthūru ʿalā ṣ-ṣundūqi l-maktūbi ʿalayhi mafrrūshāt waqtan aṭwala mimmā kāna mutawqqaʿan wa-kāda yusabbību laḥẓatan min adh-dhuʿr.
sentences.grammarNote:
**كاد يُسبِّب** uses the near-past auxiliary كاد (to nearly/almost do) followed by a bare مضارع without أن; كاد is one of the أفعال المقاربة (verbs of approximation) that take a مضارع directly as their خبر — he almost caused.
**المكتوب عليه** is a passive participle with the resumptive prepositional phrase عليه; when a passive participle modifies a noun and the action involves a preposition, the preposition + pronoun must be retained in the modifier — the box written-on-it.

sentences.base_language: By midnight, the bed was assembled and the basics were in place.
sentences.target_language: بحلول منتصف الليل، كان السرير قد رُكِّب والأساسيات في مكانها.
sentences.romanized: Bi-ḥulūli muntaṣafi l-layli, kāna s-sarīru qad rukkiba wa-l-asāsiyyātu fī makānihā.
sentences.grammarNote:
**رُكِّب** is the passive of Form II ركَّب (to assemble/install); Form II passive (فُعِّل pattern) assigns no agent and places emphasis on the completed result — the bed had been assembled — complementing كان + قد to express the past perfect.
**بحلول** is the masdar of Form I حلَّ (to arrive/set in) in the pattern فُعول used in the preposition phrase بحلول + time expression — upon the arrival of midnight; this construction is a fixed MSA temporal formula.

sentences.base_language: Sami lay down exhausted, listening to the unfamiliar sounds of the new building and street.
sentences.target_language: استلقى سامي منهَكاً، مُصغياً إلى الأصوات الغريبة للبناء والشارع الجديدَيْن.
sentences.romanized: Istalqā Sāmī munhakan, muṣghiyan ilā l-aṣwāti l-gharībati lil-binā'i wa-sh-shāriʿi l-jadīdayni.
sentences.grammarNote:
**الجديدَيْن** is the dual genitive of جديد agreeing with the coordinated nouns البناء والشارع; when two singular nouns are coordinated and share one adjective, MSA may cast the adjective in the dual to signal that both nouns are modified — the two new ones.
**مُصغياً** is a Form IV active participle (مُفعِل of صغا: أصغى, to incline the ear/listen attentively) used as a ḥāl — describing Sami's state as he lay down; the ḥāl participle is accusative and agrees with the subject.

sentences.base_language: A distant clock struck midnight and Sami finally felt something settle inside him — this was home now.
sentences.target_language: دقَّت ساعةٌ بعيدةٌ منتصفَ الليل، وأحسَّ سامي أخيراً بشيءٍ يستقرُّ في داخله — فهذا هو البيت الآن.
sentences.romanized: Daqqat sāʿatun baʿīdatun muntaṣafa l-layli, wa-aḥassa Sāmī akhīran bi-shayʾin yastaqirru fī dākhilihi — fa-hādhā huwa l-baytu l-ān.
sentences.grammarNote:
**يستقرُّ** (Form X of قرَّ) in the relative clause modifying شيء means something settling/becoming stable; the imperfect in an indefinite relative clause (صفة) describes an action coinciding with or following the main verb — he felt something settling.
**فهذا هو البيت** uses the pronoun هو as a separating pronoun (ضمير الفصل) between the subject هذا and the predicate البيت; the separating pronoun clarifies that البيت is the predicate and not a second subject, and adds emphasis — this indeed is home.

sentences.base_language: As he drifted off to sleep, he felt grateful for good friends and excited about everything the new neighborhood would bring.
sentences.target_language: وهو يغفو، شعر بالامتنان لأصدقائه الطيِّبين ومتحمِّساً لكلِّ ما سيجلبه الحيُّ الجديد.
sentences.romanized: Wa-huwa yaghfū, shaʿara bil-imtinān li-aṣdiqāʾihi ṭ-ṭayyibīna wa-mutaḥammisan li-kulli mā sa-yajlibuhu l-ḥayyu l-jadīd.
sentences.grammarNote:
**وهو يغفو** is a واو الحال (ḥāl wāw) + subject pronoun + imperfect verb, forming a nominal ḥāl clause — while/as he was drifting off to sleep; this construction is one of the most common ways to express a simultaneous circumstance in MSA narrative.
**ما سيجلبه** is a relative clause with ما as the head and the resumptive suffix ـه on يجلب; ما here is a relative pronoun meaning that which/everything that, and the suffix ـه refers back to ما as the logical object of the verb — everything the neighborhood will bring.
